'''Mose Masoe''' (born 17 May 1989) is a former [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]], [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[NRL]] and was contracted to the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], but did not feature in the first team due to serious injury. He played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] with whom he won the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]].

==Background==
Masoe was born in [[Wellington]], New Zealand. Masoe played his junior football for the [[Randwick Kingfishers]] in the [[Wellington Rugby League]] competition and the [[Wellington Orcas]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]].

==Playing career==
===Sydney Roosters (2008–12)===
Masoe was signed by the [[Sydney Roosters]]. He played for the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] team in 2008 and 2009, scoring 2 tries in 17 games.

Before moving onto the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]]' [[New South Wales Cup]] reserve-grade team, the [[Newtown Jets]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/m/masoe_mose/index.php|title=Grandstand Forums|publisher=rleague.com}}</ref>
[[File:Mose Masoe 2010.jpg|thumb|right|Masoe with the [[Sydney Roosters]] in 2010]]
In round 12 of the [[2010 NRL season]], he made his [[NRL]] début for the [[Sydney Roosters]] against the [[Gold Coast Titans]].  Masoe played 12 games for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in his debut season as the club reached the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]].  Masoe did not feature in either the club's finals campaign or the decider itself.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/the-wooden-spoon-to-premier-gold-roosters-run-recalls-league-history-20100921-15lfu.html|title=The wooden spoon to premier gold? Roosters run recalls league history|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=21 September 2010|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

Masoe was part of the [[Newtown Jets]] team that won the 2012 [[New South Wales Cup]] competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news-display/Mose-Masoe-to-join-the-Panthers/66614|title=Mose Masoe to join the Panthers|publisher=Penrith Panthers|date=7 December 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029185252/http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news-display/Mose-Masoe-to-join-the-Panthers/66614|archive-date=29 October 2013|df=dmy-all}}</ref><ref name="Stuff.co.nz_4867493">{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/the-press/sport/rugby-league/4867493/Matulino-Masoe-great-mates-off-the-field|title=Matulino, Masoe great mates off the field|author=Johnstone, Duncan|date=9 April 2011|work= [[The Press]]|access-date=26 October 2011}}</ref>

===Penrith Panthers (2013)===
On 7 December 2012, Masoe signed a two-year contract with the [[Penrith Panthers]] starting in 2013.  He played 17 games for Penrith in the [[2013 NRL season]] as the club missed out on the finals.

===St Helens (2014–15)===
On 26 June 2013, Masoe signed a 2-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], commencing from the 2014 season, after being released from the final year of his [[Penrith Panthers]]' contract.

He joined the club alongside his [[Penrith Panthers]]' team-mate [[Luke Walsh]], who had also signed a two-year contract with the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/page/3124|title=View News Article | Welcome to the Official Website of St Helens RFC|publisher=Saintsrlfc.com|date=25 June 2013|access-date=25 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029194237/http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/page/3124|archive-date=29 October 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news-display/Mose-Masoe-released-from-final-year-of-his-contract/78013|title=Mose Masoe released from final year of his contract|publisher=Penrith Panthers|date=26 June 2013}}</ref>

[[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]]. Masoe was selected to play as one of the starting {{rlp|pr}}s in their 14–6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|access-date=12 October 2014|work= [[BBC Sport]]|publisher= [[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref>

===St. George Illawarra Dragons (2016)===
On 29 June 2015, Masoe signed a two-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] ahead of the start of the [[2016 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2015/06/29/dragons_sign_mose_ma.html|title=Dragons Sign Mose Masoe|website=dragons.com.au|date=29 June 2015|access-date=26 September 2018}}</ref>

However, an [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injury kept Masoe out for the entire 2016 [[rugby league]] season.  
[[File:Mose Masoe.jpg|thumb|right|Masoe playing for St. George Illawarra in 2017]]
Subsequently in 2017, he was released without having played in a single game for [[St. George Illawarra]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-transfer-centre-every-player-movement-ahead-of-2018-season/news-story/77ee88e23d3a0a061174354a15eff914|title=NRL 2018 Transfer Centre: Every player movement|date=27 November 2017|url-access=subscription}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2017–2020)===
Masoe penned a deal to join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the remainder of the 2017 [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] season, with the option to extend his contract for the 2018 season based on the club gaining promotion back to the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/hull-kr-sign-mose-masoe-214700|title=Hull KR new signing Mose Masoe says he's returning to England a better player|publisher=Hull Daily Mail|date=19 July 2017|access-date=17 January 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/mose-masoe-way-hull-kr-256674|title=Mose Masoe on his way to Hull KR as Rovers Supporters Group helps pay for flight costs|publisher=Hull Daily Mail|date=31 July 2017|access-date=17 January 2018}}</ref>

Masoe made his [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' début on 6 August 2017, in a 26–22 [[The Qualifiers|Qualifiers]] victory over [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40793616|title=The Qualifiers: Hull KR 26-22 Halifax|work=BBC Sport|date=6 August 2017|access-date=26 September 2018}}</ref>

Masoe was part of the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' side that gained automatic promotion back to the [[Super League]] in the 2017 season.  It was revealed on 26 June 2018, that Mose had signed a new three-year contract extension to stay at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] until at least the end of the 2021 [[rugby league]] season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44622105|title=Mose Masoe: Hull KR prop signs new three-year contract |work=BBC Sport|date=26 June 2018|access-date=26 September 2018}}</ref>

In January 2020, Masoe was taken from the field on a stretcher during the club's pre-season friendly against [[Wakefield Trinity]] after suffering a serious spinal injury.  Masoe was later rushed to hospital where he had emergency surgery.  On 16 January 2020, it was announced that Masoe would not play [[rugby league]] again and was forced into early retirement.  Hull KR head coach [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] spoke to the media saying “This is a career-ending injury. If Mose is able to walk again that will be a success.  I don’t want to paint a picture that everything is going to be fine and he’s going to be back doing things he used to do. It’s going to be a long old haul for Mose".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51141749|title=Mose Masoe: Hull KR forward will not play again after serious spinal injury|work=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-prop-mose-masoe-21295638/|title=Hull KR prop Mose Masoe forced to retire after serious spinal injury in Wakefield match|website=www.mirror.co.uk}}</ref> During Masoe’s injury rehabilitation, he was able to first take unaided steps in September 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.itv.com/news/calendar/2020-09-23/mose-masoe-takes-his-first-steps-unaided-just-eight-months-after-suffering-a-serious-spinal-injury|title= Mose Masoe takes his first steps unaided just eight months after suffering a serious spinal injury|website=ITV.com|date= 24 September 2020}}</ref>

==Representative career (2010–14)==
Masoe has previously played for the Junior Kiwis.  During the 2010 season, Masoe was selected in the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] train-on squads.  During the 2013 season, Masoe was selected in the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] train-on squads once again, subsequently opting to play for [[Samoa]].

Due to Masoe's exceptional performances in the 2013 season, he was selected in the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] squad and made his début at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.wsamoa.ws/index.php?m=25&s=&i=9789|title=Samoa Online|publisher=Wsamoa.ws|date=15 June 2007}}</ref><ref name="Stuff.co.nz_4101582">{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/4101582/NRL-finalists-bulk-out-Kiwis-squad|title=NRL finalists bulk out Kiwis squad|date=6 September 2010|agency= [[NZPA]]|work= [[Stuff.co.nz]]|access-date=26 October 2011}}</ref>

On 7 October 2014, Masoe was selected in [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]]'s 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] series.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82434/default.aspx|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|date=7 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/ |title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad |publisher=Zero Tackle |access-date=2016-03-01 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203031215/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/ |archive-date=3 February 2016 |df=dmy-all }}</ref>
